page: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. Storage is network-based with a capacity of 1 GB, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded execution. The server achieves top-tier status in single-core stress-ng CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression at 39.96 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.65 GB/sec. However, due to its shared CPU allocation and dual-thread single-core design, multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is generally weak, with read-only large block speeds at 29.73 GB/sec, though write speeds maintain an average tier at 28.99 GB/sec.

Database operations are a notable bottleneck, with PassMark database operations scoring in the bottom 10% and Redis SET operations performing in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput is average at 8.04 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning, text generation on a small 135M model is average at 106.45 tokens/sec, but drops significantly for larger models. This profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic development environments, while being poorly suited for heavy database workloads or intensive multi-threaded computing.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a shared-CPU virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 vCPUs (1 physical AMD EPYC core with 2 threads) clocked at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage. It exhibits strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for single-threaded stress-ng, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. However, its shared resource model limits multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, placing them in the bottom 25%. Database operations are particularly constrained, with PassMark database benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10%. This server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, static web serving, and basic development tasks rather than database-heavy or highly concurrent workloads.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a VX1 Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, optimized for single-threaded workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g is a shared-CPU virtual server with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server delivering strong single-threaded execution and balanced memory for lightweight web serving and development tasks.
bullet_points: Features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor at 2.0 GHz.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 1 GB of network-attached storage.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Shows performance limitations in multi-core scaling and database operations.
Best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and development.
